accurate.why: All claims match the bill text, and the added context that the Secretary is the Secretary of Energy administering the RMUC program is verified as correct.

no_hallucinations.why: The only added context (Secretary of Energy / DOE administration) is accurate and verified; nothing is fabricated.

states_purpose.why: The summary clearly states the bill reauthorizes and revises the Rural and Municipal Utility Advanced Cybersecurity Grant and Technical Assistance Program under IIJA Section 40124.

changes_to_existing_law.why: It correctly identifies that the bill amends Section 40124 of the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act to reauthorize and revise the program.

exceptions_conditions.why: It captures key eligibility limits (e.g., investor-owned utilities under 4M MWh, not-for-profits partnered with at least six entities) and the FOIA disclosure exemption.

effective_dates.why: It correctly notes the FY2026-2030 authorization period; the bill specifies no other dates or deadlines.

major_provisions_covered.why: It covers the program maintenance, objectives, eligible entities, award mechanisms, priority criteria, information protection, and appropriations.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and descriptive with no advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: The summary is well-organized, logically structured, and internally consistent.

concise.why: The summary is appropriately succinct without padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: The Secretary (of Energy), IIJA Section 40124, the RMUC program, and the FOIA are all correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: The $250,000,000 figure, FY2026-2030 period, and 4,000,000 MWh threshold all match the bill text.
